alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels. Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants to ensure food safety. These levels are legally permitted maximum concentrations. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Labelling Requirement - Source fetch / extraction issue
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Manual review required. Automated extraction failed: {"error":{"code":503,"message":"This model is currently experiencing high demand. Spikes in demand are usually temporary. Please try again later.","status":"UNAVAILABLE"}}

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Additive Limits - General requirements for Halal food, including permissible additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required.

alerts.title: Added: GCC/GSO Labelling Requirement - Labelling requirements for Halal food
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance with GSO 9:2022. This technical regulation sets out the general requirements for labelling of prepackaged food. Specific details are within the document.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Labelling Requirement - Mandatory expiration dates
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Compliance with GSO 150-1:2013. This technical regulation specifies mandatory expiration date requirements for food products.

alerts.title: Added: GCC Microbiology Limits - Microbiological criteria
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required as per GSO 1016:2015. This technical regulation specifies microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients. The specific limits are contained within the full document.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Additive Limits - Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited unless specified as unlikely to cause harm by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. This establishes a positive list principle for additives.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Contamination or foreign substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if likely to cause harm due to contamination or addition of foreign substances. This is a general prohibition against harmful contamination.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Toxic or harmful substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited, unless specified as harmless by MHLW. This applies to substances contained in or borne by food or additives.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Microbiology Limits - Pathogens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited if contaminated with pathogens and likely to cause harm. This applies to food or additives contaminated with pathogens.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Compliance with regulatory limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be assessed for safety by SFA. Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Compliance with regulatory limits for contaminants
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory. All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents (contaminants) in food.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (incidental constituents)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be within SFA's regulatory limits. Contaminants are substances present in food that can occur during production, manufacturing, treatment, packing, packaging, transport, or holding, or as a result of environmental contamination.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants exceeding limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibition of import or sale. If laboratory test results indicate contaminants exceed regulatory limits, the food product must not be imported or sold.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Veterinary drug residues (diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, dienoestrol)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Prohibition applies to the import or sale of meat and any food derived from meat containing these specific compounds.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Declaration of ingredients and additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory declaration. Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Prohibited Foods
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibition. Certain foods are prohibited from production, import, or sale; specific list must be verified.

alerts.title: Added: United States Additive Limits - Prohibited substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from use. Substances listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food are not allowed.

alerts.title: Added: United States Allergen List - Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. These 9 allergens are recognized by U.S. law (FALCPA and FASTER Act).

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 1. This is a Guidance Level.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. This is an Action Level.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. This is an Action Level.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. This is an Action Level.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Poisonous or deleterious substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Contaminant levels (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, etc.) for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food are published in various guidance documents and regulations. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509. The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Allergen cross-contact and undeclared allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Facilities must implement allergen preventive controls and written procedures. The CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate labeling.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Cochineal extract and carmine
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be declared on the label. Identified as allergenic substances derived from insects.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of major food allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must appear immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a 'contains' statement. Example: 'Contains wheat, milk, and soy.'

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of specific tree nut, fish, and Crustacean shellfish
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Type of tree nut, species of fish, and species of Crustacean shellfish must be declared. Examples: almonds, pecans, walnuts for tree nuts; bass, flounder, cod for fish; crab, lobster, shrimp for Crustacean shellfish.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - FD&C Yellow No. 5
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be identified on labels. Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Gluten-free claim
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must meet defined standard for gluten content. A final rule defines 'gluten-free' for food labeling. Compliance requirements also exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Sulfiting agents (e.g., sodium bisulfite)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be declared when concentration is ≥10 total sulfites. Required due to adverse reactions, such as asthma, in sensitive individuals.

alerts.title: Removed: China Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required against GB 2760-2015. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Additive Limits - Specific food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required via GSFA database. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets conditions for permitted food additive use in all foods. Users should consult the Preamble for interpretation. The database is regularly updated.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives

alerts.details: New: Verification required via CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices. A specific Code of Practice exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices, providing guidance to minimize contamination.

alerts.details: New: Must conform to criteria/standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.. The specific criteria and standards are established by MHLW and must be checked.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Additive Limits - Food Additives

alerts.details: New: Must not exceed quantities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm. Verification required.. This refers to the 'Positive List System' for residues. Specific limits are set by MHLW.
